author: Augustine of Hippo
book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.

ccel.title: NPNF1-07. St. Augustine: Homilies on the Gospel of John; Homilies on the First Epistle of John; Soliloquies

source.title: NPNF1-07. St. Augustine: Homilies on the Gospel of John; Homilies on the First Epistle of John; Soliloquies

title: Chapter VII. 1–13.

source.title: NPNF1-07. St. Augustine: Homilies on the Gospel of John; Homilies on the First Epistle of John; Soliloquies

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: In this chapter of the Gospel, brethren, our Lord Jesus Christ has most especially commended Himself to our faith in respect of His humanity.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For indeed He always keeps in view, both in His words and deeds, that He should be believed to be God and man: God who made us, man who sought us; with the Father, always God; with us, man in time.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For He would not have sought man whom He had made if Himself had not become that which He had made.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But remember this, and do not let it slip from your hearts, that Christ became man in such manner that He ceased not to be God.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: While remaining God, He who made man took manhood.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: While, therefore, as man He concealed Himself, He must not be thought to have lost His power, but only to have offered an example to our infirmity.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For He was detained when He willed to be, and He was put to death when he willed to be.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But since there were to be His members, that is, His faithful ones, who would not have that power which He, our God, had; by His being hid, by His con cealing Himself as if He would not be put to death, He indicated that His members would do this, in which members He Himself in fact was.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For Christ is not simply in the head and not in the body, but Christ whole is in the head and body.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What, therefore, His members are, that He is; but what He is, it does not necessarily follow that His members are.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For if His members were not Himself, He would not have said, “Saul, why persecutest thou me?”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For Saul was not persecuting Himself on earth, but His members, namely, His believers.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He would not, however, say, my saints, my servants, or, in short, my brethren, which is more honorable; but, me, that is, my members, whose head I am.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: With these preliminary remarks, I think that we shall not have to labor much for the meaning in this chapter; for that is often betokened in the head which was to be in the body.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “After these things,” saith he, “Jesus walked in Galilee: for He would not walk in Judea, because the Jews sought to kill Him.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: This is what I have said; He offered an example to our infirmity.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He had not lost power, but He was comforting our weakness.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For it would happen, as I have said, that some believer in Him would retreat into concealment, lest he should be found by the persecutors; and lest the concealment should be objected to him as a crime, that occurred first in the head, which should afterwards be confirmed in the member.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For it is said, “He would not walk in Judea, because the Jews sought to kill Him,” just as if Christ were not able both to walk among the Jews, and not be killed by them.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For He manifested this power when He willed; for when they would lay hold of Him, as He was now about to suffer, “He said to them, Whom seek ye?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: They answered, Jesus.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Then, said He, I am He,” not concealing, but manifesting Himself.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: That manifestation, however, they did not withstand, but “going backwards, they fell to the ground.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And yet, because He had come to suffer, they rose up, laid hold of Him, led Him away to the judge, and slew Him.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But what was it they did?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: That which a certain scripture says: “The earth was delivered into the hands of the ungodly.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The flesh was given into the power of the Jews; and this that thereby the bag, as it were, might be rent asunder, whence our purchase-price might run out.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “Now the Jews’ feast of tabernacles was at hand.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What the feast of tabernacles is, they who read the Scriptures know.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: They used on the holy day to make tabernacles, in likeness of the tabernacles in which they dwelt while they sojourned in the wilderness, after being led out of Egypt.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: This was a holy day, a great solemnity.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The Jews were celebrating this, as being mindful of the Lord’s benefits—they who were about to kill the Lord.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: On this holy day, then (for there were several holy days; but it was called a holy day with the Jews, though it was not one day, but several), “His brethren” spoke to the Lord Christ.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Understand the phrase, “His brethren,” as you know it must be taken, for it is not a new thing you hear.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The blood relations of the Virgin Mary used to be called the Lord’s brethren.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For it was of the usage of Scripture to call blood relations and all other near kindred by the term brethren, which is foreign to our usage, and not within our manner of speech.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For who would call an uncle or a sister’s son “brother”?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Yet the Scripture calls relatives of this kind “brothers.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For Abraham and Lot are called brothers, while Abraham was Lot’s uncle.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Laban and Jacob are called brothers, while Laban was Jacob’s uncle.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: When, therefore, you hear of the Lord’s brethren, consider them the blood relations of Mary, who did not a second time bear children.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For, as in the sepulchre, where the Lord’s body was laid, neither before nor after did any dead lie; so, likewise, Mary’s womb, neither before nor after conceived anything mortal.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: We have said who the brethren were, let us hear what they said: “Pass over hence, and go into Judea, that thy disciples also may see thy work which thou doest.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The Lord’s works were not hid from the disciples, but to these men they were not apparent.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: They might have Christ for a kinsman, but through that very relationship they disdained to believe on Him.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: It is told us in the Gospel; for we dare not hold this as a mere opinion, you have just now heard it.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: They go on advising Him: “For no man doeth anything in secret, and he himself seeketh to be known openly: if thou do these things, show thyself to the world.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And directly after it says: “For neither did His brethren believe in Him.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Why did they not believe in Him?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Because they sought human glory.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For as to what His brethren appear to advise Him, they consult for His glory.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Thou doest marvellous works, make thyself known; that is, appear to all, that thou mayest be praised by all.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The flesh spoke to the flesh; but the flesh without God, to the flesh with God.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: It was the wisdom of the flesh speak ing to the Word which became flesh and dwelt among us.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What did the Lord answer to these things?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Then saith Jesus to them: “My time is not yet come; but your time is always ready.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What is this?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Had not Christ’s time yet come?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Why then was Christ come, if His time had not yet come?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Have we not heard the apostle say, “But when the fullness of time came, God sent His Son”?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If, therefore, He was sent in the fullness of time, He was sent when He ought to be sent, He came when it behoved that He should come.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What means then, “My time is not yet come”?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Understand, brethren, with what intention they spoke, when they appeared to advise Him as their brother.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: They were giving Him counsel to pursue glory; as advising in a worldly manner and with an earthly disposition, that He should not be unknown to fame, nor hide Himself in obscurity.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: This is what the Lord says in answer to those who were giving Him counsel of glory, “My time is not yet come;”—the time of my glory is not yet come.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: See how profound it is: they were advising Him as to glory; but He would have loftiness preceded by humility, and willed to prepare the way to elevation itself through humility.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For those disciples, too, were of course seeking glory who wished to sit, one at His right hand and the other at His left: they thought only of the goal, and saw not by what way it must be reached; the Lord recalled them to the way, that they might come to their fatherland in due order.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For the fatherland is on high, the way thither lies low.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: That land is the life of Christ, the way is Christ’s death; that land is the habitation of Christ, the way is Christ’s suffering.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He that refuses the way, why seeks he the fatherland?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: In a word, to these also, while seeking elevation, He gave this answer: “Can ye drink the cup which I am about to drink?”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Behold the way by which you must come to that height which you desire.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The cup He made mention of was indeed that of His humility and suffering.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Therefore also here: “My time is not yet come; but your time,” that is the glory of the world, “is always ready.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: This is the time of which Christ, that is the body of Christ, speaks in prophecy: “When I shall have received the fit time, I will judge righteously.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For at present it is not the time of judging, but of tolerating the wicked.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Therefore, let the body of Christ bear at present, and tolerate the wickedness of evil livers.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Let it, however, have righteousness now, for by righteousness it shall come to judgment.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And what saith the Holy Scripture in the psalm to the members,—namely, that tolerate the wickedness of this world?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “The Lord will not cast off His people.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For, in fact, His people labors among the unworthy, among the unrighteous, among blasphemers, among murmurers, detractors, persecutors, and, if they are allowed, destroyers.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Yes, it labors; but “the Lord will not cast off His people, and He will not forsake His inheritance until justice is turned into judgment.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “Until the justice,” which is now in His saints, “be turned into judgment;” when that shall be fulfilled which was said to them, “Ye shall sit upon twelve thrones, judging the twelve tribes of Israel.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The apostle had righteousness, but not yet that judgment of which he says, “Know ye not that we shall judge angels?”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Be it now, therefore, the time for living rightly; the time for judging them that have lived ill shall be hereafter.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “Until righteousness,” saith he, “is turned into judgment.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The time of judgment will be that of which the Lord has here said, “My time is not yet come.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For there will be a time of glory, when He who came in humility will come in loftiness; He who came to be judged will come to judge; He who came to be slain by the dead will come to judge the quick and the dead.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “God,” saith the psalm, “will come manifest, our God, and He will not be silent.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What is “shall come manifest”?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Because He came concealed.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Then He will not be silent; for when He came concealed, “He was led as a sheep to the slaughter; and as a lamb before its shearer, He opened not His mouth.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He shall come, and shall not keep silence.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “I was silent,” saith He, “shall I always be silent?”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But what is necessary at the present time for those who have righteousness?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: That which is read in that psalm: “Until righteousness is turned into judgment, and they that have it are upright of heart.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: You ask, perhaps, who are the upright in heart?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: We find in Scripture those to be upright in heart who bear the evils of the world, and do not accuse God.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: See, brethren, an uncommon thing is that which I speak of.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For I know not how it is that, when any evil befalls a man, he runs to accuse God, when he ought to accuse himself.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: When thou gettest any good, thou praisest thyself; when thou sufferest any evil, thou accusest God.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: This is then the crooked heart, not the upright.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: When thou art cured of this distorting and perversity, what thou didst use to do will be turned into the contrary.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For what didst thou use to do before?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Thou didst praise thyself in the good things of God, and didst accuse God in thine own evil things; with thy heart converted and made right, thou wilt praise God in His good things, and accuse thyself in thy own evil things.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: These are the upright in heart.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: In short, that man, who was not yet right in heart when the success of the wicked and the distress of the good grieved him, says, when he is corrected: “How good is the God of Israel to the upright in heart!

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But as for me,” when I was not right in heart, “my feet were almost gone; my steps had well-nigh slipped.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “Because I was envious at sinners, beholding the peace of sinners.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: I saw, saith he, the wicked prosperous, and I was displeased at God; for I did wish that God should not permit the wicked to be happy.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Let man understand: God never does permit this; but a bad man is thought to be happy, for this reason, because men are ignorant of what happiness is.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Let us then be right in heart: the time of our glory is not yet come.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Let it be told to the lovers of this world, such as the brethren of the Lord were, “your time is always ready;” our time “is not yet come.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For let us, too, dare to say this.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And since we are the body of our Lord Jesus Christ, since we are His members, since we joyfully acknowledge our head, let us say it without hesitation; since, for our sakes, He deigned also Himself to say this.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And when the lovers of this world revile us, let us say to them, “Your time is always ready; our time is not yet come.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For the apostle has said to us, “For ye are dead, and your life is hid with Christ in God.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: When will our time come?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “When Christ,” saith he, “your life shall appear, then shall ye also appear with Him in glory.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What said He further?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “The world cannot hate you.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What is this, but, The world cannot hate its lovers, the false witnesses?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For you call the things that are evil, good; and the things that are good, evil.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “But me it hateth, because I bear witness concerning it, that its works are evil.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Go ye up to this feast.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What means “to this”?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Where ye seek human glory.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What means “to this”?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Where ye wish to prolong carnal joys, not to meditate on eternal joys.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “I go not up to this feast, because my time is not yet full come.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: On this feast-day you seek human glory; but my time, that is, the time of my glory, is not yet come.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: That will be my feast-day, not running before and passing over these days, but remaining for ever; that will be festivity, joy without end, eternity without a blot, serenity without a cloud.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “When He had said these words unto them, He abode still in Galilee.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But when His brethren were gone up, then went He also up unto the feast, not openly, but as it were in secret.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Therefore “not to this feast-day,” because His desire was not for temporal glory, but to teach something to profit, to correct men, to admonish them of an eternal feast-day, to turn away their love from this world, and to turn it to God.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But what means this, “He went up as it were in secret to the feast”?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: This action of the Lord also is not without meaning.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: It appears to me that, even from this circumstance that He went up as it were in secret, He had intended to signify something; for the things that follow will show that He thus went up on the middle of the feast, that is, when those days were half over, to teach even openly.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But he said, “As it were in secret,” meaning, not to show Himself to men.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: It is not without meaning that Christ went up “as it were in secret” to that feast, because He Himself lay hid in that feast-day.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What I have said as yet is also under cover of secrecy.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Let it be manifested then, let the veil be lifted, and let that which was secret appear.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: All things that were spoken to the ancient people Israel in the manifold Scripture of the holy law, what things they did, whether in sacrifices, or in priestly offices, or in feast-days, and, in a word, in what things soever they worshipped God, what things soever were spoken to and given them in precept, were shadows of things to come.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Of what things to come?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Things which find their fulfillment in Christ.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Whence the apostle says, “For all the promises of God are in Him yea;” that is, they are fulfilled in Him.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Again he says in another place, “All happened to them in a figure; but they were written for our sakes, upon whom the end of the ages is come.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And he said elsewhere, “For Christ is the end of the law;” likewise in another place, “Let no man judge you in meat, or in drink, or in respect of an holy day, or of a new moon, or of Sabbath-days, which is a shadow of things to come.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If, therefore, all these things were shadows of things to come, also the feast of tabernacles was a shadow of things to come.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Let us examine, then, of what thing to come was this feast-day a shadow.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: I have explained what this feast of tabernacles was: it was a celebration of taber nacles, because the people, after their deliverance from Egypt, while directing their course through the wilderness to the land of promise, dwelt in tents.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Let us observe what it is, and we shall be that thing; we, I say, who are members of Christ, if such we are; but we are, He having made us worthy, not we having earned it for ourselves.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Let us then consider ourselves, brethren: we have been led out of Egypt, where we were slaves to the devil as to Pharaoh; where we applied ourselves to works of clay, engaged in earthly desires, and where we toiled exceedingly.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And to us, while laboring, as it were, at the bricks, Christ cried aloud, “Come unto me, all ye that labor and are heavy laden.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Thence we were led out by baptism as through the Red Sea,—red because consecrated by the blood of Christ.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: All our enemies that pursued us being dead, that is, all our sins being blotted out, we have been brought over to the other side.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: At the present time, then, before we come to the land of promise, namely, the eternal kingdom, we are in the wilderness in tabernacles.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: They who acknowledge these things are in tabernacles; for it was to be that some would acknowledge this.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For that man, who understands that he is a sojourner in this world, is in tabernacles.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: That man understands that he is travelling in a foreign country, when he sees himself sighing for his native land.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But whilst the body of Christ is in tabernacles, Christ is in tabernacles; but at that time He was so, not evidently but secretly.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For as yet the shadow obscured the light; when the light came, the shadow was removed.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Christ was in secret: He was in the feast of tabernacles, but there hidden.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: At the present time, when these things are already made manifest, we acknowledge that we are journeying in the wilderness: for if we know it, we are in the wilderness.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What is it to be in the wilderness?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: In the desert waste.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Why in the desert waste?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Because in this world, where we thirst in a way in which is no water.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But yet, let us thirst that we may be filled.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For, “Blessed are they that hunger and thirst after righteousness, for they shall be filled.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And our thirst is quenched from the rock in the wilderness: for “the Rock was Christ,” and it was smitten with a rod that the water might flow.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But that it might flow, the rock was smitten twice: because there are two beams of the cross.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: All these things, then, which were done in a figure, are made manifest to us.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And it is not without meaning that it was said of the Lord, “He went up to the feast-day, but not openly, but as it were in secret.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For Himself in secret was the thing prefigured, because Christ was hid in that same festal-day; for that very festal-day signified Christ’s members that were to sojourn in a foreign land.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “Then the Jews sought Him on the feast-day:” before He went up.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For His brethren went up before Him, and He went not up then when they supposed and wished: that this too might be fulfilled which He said, “Not to this, that is, the first or second day, to which you wish me to go.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But He went up afterwards, as the Gospel tells us, “on the middle of the feast;’ that is, when as many days of that feast had passed as there remained.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For they celebrated that same festival, so far we can understand, on several successive days.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “They said, therefore, Where is he?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And there was much murmuring among the people concerning Him.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Whence the murmuring?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What was the strife?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “Some said, He is a good man; but others said, Nay; but he deceiveth the people.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: We must understand this of all His servants: this is said now of them.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For whoever becomes eminent in some spiritual grace, of him some will assuredly say, “He is a good man;” others, “Nay; but he deceiveth the people.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Whence is this?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “Because our life is hid with Christ in God.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: On this account people may say during the winter, This tree is dead; for example, a fig tree, pear tree, or some kind of fruit tree, it is like a withered tree, and so long as it is winter it does not appear whether it is so or not.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But the summer proves, the judgment proves.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Our summer is the appearing of Christ: “God shall come manifest, our God, and He will not be silent;” “fire shall go before Him:” that fire “shall burn up His enemies:” that fire shall lay hold of the withered trees.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For then shall the dry trees be apparent, when it shall be said to them, “I was hungry, and ye gave me not to eat;” but on the other side, namely, on the right, will be seen abundance of fruit, and magnificence of leaves; the green will be eternity.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: To those, then, as withered trees, it shall be said, “Go into everlasting fire.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For behold,” it saith, “the axe is laid to the root of the trees: every tree, therefore, that bringeth not forth good fruit shall be cut down, and cast into the fire.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Let them then say of thee, if thou art growing in Christ, let men say of thee, “He deceiveth the people.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: This is said of Christ Himself; it is said of the whole body of Christ.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Think of the body of Christ still in the world, think of it still on the threshing-floor; see how it is blasphemed by the chaff.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The chaff and the grain are, indeed, threshed together; but the chaff is consumed, the corn is purged.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What was said of the Lord then, avails for consolation, whenever it will be said of any Christian.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “Howbeit no man spake openly of Him for fear of the Jews.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But who were they that did not speak of Him for fear of the Jews?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Undoubtedly they who said, “He is a good man:” not they who said, “He deceiveth the people.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: As for them who said “He deceiveth the people,” their din was heard like the noise of dry leaves.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “He deceiveth the people,” they sounded more and more loudly: “He is a good man,” they whispered more and more constrainedly.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But now, brethren, notwithstanding that glory of Christ which is to make us immortal is not yet come, yet now, I say, His Church so increases, He has deigned to spread it abroad through the whole world, that it is now only whispered.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “He deceiveth the people;” and more and more loudly it sounds forth, “He is a good man.”
